name: The four bridges of the Guadalquivir River — circular tour of the city of Sevilla
name_original: Los cuatro puentes del Río Guadalquivir — circular por la ciudad de Sevilla
region_intro: Popular urban hiking trails around the Guadalquivir river traverse a region characterized by its diverse landscapes, including fertile plains, extensive marshes, and the river's distinct meanders. The area features significant riparian habitats along the riverbanks, which are important ecological corridors within urban settings. While the Sierra Morena foothills influence the northern parts, urban routes primarily focus on the river and city environments. This blend of natural riverine features and urban development offers varied walking experiences. Best urban hiking trails around the Guadalquivir The most popular urban hiking route is Alcazar of Seville, Cathedral of Santa Maria and Setas de Sevilla - walk through the old town - circular, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 15 minutes to complete. This easy route explores Seville's historic old town and its iconic landmarks. Another top favourite among local hikers is The four bridges of the Guadalquivir River — circular tour of the city of Sevilla, an easy 3.7 miles (5.9 km) path. This route offers views of the river and the city's bridges, providing a scenic urban experience. Local hikers also love the Plaza of Spain – Fountain of Plaza de España loop from Seville, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail leading through urban parks and historic plazas, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around the Guadalquivir is defined by riverine paths, urban parks, and historic city centers, offering a blend of natural and cultural exploration. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate urban treks. The routes in Guadalquivir are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 1200 reviews. More than 10300 hikers have used komoot to explore Guadalquivir's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many urban hiking trails are available around the Guadalquivir?
A: There are over 300 urban hiking trails around the Guadalquivir, catering to various preferences. Most of these, around 220, are considered easy, while about 80 are moderate, offering a good range for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on urban hikes along the Guadalquivir?
A: Urban hikes along the Guadalquivir primarily feature flat, paved paths and riverside promenades, ideal for easy walking. Some routes may venture into areas with gentle inclines, especially where the river approaches the foothills of the Sierra Morena, but generally, the terrain is accessible and well-maintained within city limits.

Q: When is the best time of year for urban hiking along the Guadalquivir?
A: The mild Mediterranean climate makes urban hiking along the Guadalquivir enjoyable for much of the year. The best times are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are pleasant. Winters are relatively mild, but it's best to avoid the peak summer months (July and August) due to high heat.

Q: Are there any family-friendly urban hikes along the Guadalquivir?
A: Yes, many urban trails along the Guadalquivir are perfect for families. They often feature flat, accessible paths and pass by parks and attractions. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010585269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular tour of the city of Sevilla, crossing the four bridges of the Guadalquivir River&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views and easy walking for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on urban hikes around the Guadalquivir?
A: Many urban areas and riverside paths along the Guadalquivir are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is on a leash. Always check local regulations for specific parks or protected areas. Generally, open urban trails are suitable for walks with your canine companion.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see on urban hikes around the Guadalquivir?
A: Urban hikes along the Guadalquivir offer a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. In Seville, you can explore routes that pass by iconic landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seville Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Gold&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1055590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Alcázar of Seville&lt;/a&gt;. In Córdoba, trails often lead past the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mosque–Cathedral of Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3176628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Christian Monarchs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular urban hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular urban hiking routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010605256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazar of Seville, Cathedral of Santa Maria and Setas de Sevilla walk&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route through the old town. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21847562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza of Spain – Fountain of Plaza de España loop from Seville&lt;/a&gt;, offering a comprehensive city experience.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about urban hiking in Guadalquivir?
A: The urban hiking trails around the Guadalquivir are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars. Reviewers often praise the beautiful riverside views, the ease of access to historical city centers, and the well-maintained paths that make for a pleasant walking experience.

Q: Are there urban hikes that include viewpoints or scenic overlooks?
A: Absolutely. Many urban hikes along the Guadalquivir offer fantastic viewpoints, particularly those that follow the riverbanks or cross its bridges. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010585269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;four bridges of the Guadalquivir River tour in Seville&lt;/a&gt; provides numerous scenic perspectives of the city and its iconic structures like the Torre del Oro.

Q: How can I find parking or public transport for urban hikes?
A: Most urban hiking trails along the Guadalquivir are easily accessible by public transport, especially in cities like Seville and Córdoba. Information on specific bus or metro stops near trailheads can often be found on local transport websites. For parking, city centers usually have public parking garages, though availability and cost can vary. Planning ahead is recommended, particularly during peak tourist seasons.

Q: Are there cafes or places to eat near the urban hiking routes?
A: Given the urban nature of these trails, you'll find an abundance of cafes, restaurants, and tapas bars conveniently located along or very close to most routes. Cities like Seville and Córdoba are renowned for their culinary scene, so you'll have plenty of options to refuel and enjoy local delicacies during or after your hike.

Q: What is the typical distance and duration for urban hikes in this region?
A: Urban hikes around the Guadalquivir vary in length and duration. Many popular routes range from 5 to 15 kilometers, taking anywhere from 1.5 to 4 hours to complete at a moderate pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19391286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Córdoba – Mosque–Cathedral of Córdoba loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 12.8 km and takes around 3.5 hours.
tips:
Also called Triana, as it joins the city center with the Triana neighborhood and replaces an old boat bridge that existed in its place in the 19th century.
It was built in 1852, with a design by French engineers Steinacher and Bernadet based on stone and iron. It is the oldest preserved iron bridge in Spain and would be declared a National Historic Monument in 1976, being restored a year later.

Many expect a tower similar in size to Big Ben, only to be surprised by the building's low height. However, if there's one thing that's undeniable, it's that few images shine as brightly in our city as the view of this building on the banks of the Guadalquivir River, with the Maestranza and Giralda towers behind it. You're moved every time you see it at night from the San Telmo Bridge. Its height is 36 meters, and it's a watchtower, meaning it was part of a fortified enclosure, with which it was connected. Its main function was to defend the city.
It is said that its name comes from the golden glow the tower reflected on the river.
Enjoy a drink under one of the parasols by the river, a nice break away from the hustle and bustle
Bridge over the old riverbed of the Guadalquivir that connects the Paseo de las Delicias with the Remedios neighborhood. Until 2000 it was the bridge of the Generalissimo.

The Real Parroquia de Santa Ana de Sevilla is a Gothic-Mudejar style parish church, declared an Asset of Cultural Interest, whose construction began at the end of the 13th century. It is located in the neighborhood of Triana. It is popularly known as the Cathedral of Triana.
Monument "Triana al arte flamenco", a woman with a guitar, dedicated to flamenco art.

waypoints.name: Paseo de las Delicias Along the Guadalquivir River
